brief_justification: Paper A's machinery is variance-decomposition of a linear model's predictors plus doubly-stochastic matrix balancing (Sinkhorn-Knopp / alternating projections) to remove a row-sum bias in reallocation; Paper B's machinery is count-dependent differential privacy (zCDP), designing a shifted-log Gaussian mechanism and proving matching Renyi-divergence upper/lower bounds for the optimal n^-2 privacy-budget rate. There is no role-for-role correspondence: matrix balancing for regression importance and minimax mechanism design for privacy budgets are different processes, with only generic overlaps (both involve 'bias/error' and 'Gaussian/optimization') rather than shared transferable machinery.
